mission.title: Install and Configure Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP)
mission.description: Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform (OCP) is a powerful Kubernetes distribution that provides a comprehensive platform for developing and managing containerized applications. It is ideal for enterprises looking for a robust solution with built-in CI/CD, monitoring, and security features. However, it may require more resources and has a steeper learning curve compared to simpler Kubernetes distributions.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the OpenShift CLI (oc) which is necessary for interacting with your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

```

> This command downloads the latest OpenShift CLI.
commands: curl -LO https://mirror.openshift.com/pub/openshift-v4/clients/ocp/latest/oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && chmod +x oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) && sudo mv oc-linux-$(uname -s)-$(uname -m) /usr/local/bin/oc

steps.title: Create the OpenShift Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates an OpenShift cluster using the OpenShift Installer.

```bash

openshift-install create cluster --dir=cluster --log-level=info

```

> Make sure to configure the install-config.yaml file in the cluster directory before running this command.

steps.title: Set up kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to access the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

export KUBECONFIG=$(pwd)/cluster/auth/kubeconfig

```

> This sets the KUBECONFIG environment variable to point to your cluster's kubeconfig.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step enables monitoring and autoscaling features in your OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-monitoring-operator/master/deploy/cluster-monitoring-config.yaml

oc apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/openshift/cluster-autoscaler/master/deploy/cluster-autoscaler-config.yaml

```

> This command sets up the monitoring configuration.
> This command sets up the autoscaler configuration.

uninstall.title: Remove the OpenShift Cluster
uninstall.description:
This step will remove the OpenShift cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will lead to data loss.

```bash

openshift-install destroy cluster --dir=cluster --log-level=info

```

uninstall.title: Clean Up Orphaned Resources
uninstall.description:
This step ensures that any orphaned resources such as load balancers and PVCs are removed.

```bash

gcloud compute addresses delete <ADDRESS_NAME> --region=<REGION>

```

> Replace <ADDRESS_NAME> and <REGION> with your specific values.

upgrade.title: Backup Cluster Data
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure that you backup your cluster data.

```bash

oc adm must-gather --image=quay.io/openshift-release-dev/ocp-v4.0-art-dev:latest

```

> This command gathers all necessary data for backup.

upgrade.title: Drain and Cordon Nodes
upgrade.description:
This step prepares the nodes for upgrade by draining and cordoning them.

```bash

oc adm drain <NODE_NAME> --ignore-daemonsets --delete-local-data

oc adm cordon <NODE_NAME>

```

> Replace <NODE_NAME> with the name of your node.
> This prevents new pods from being scheduled on the node.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Cluster
upgrade.description:
This step performs the actual upgrade of the OpenShift cluster.

```bash

oc adm upgrade --to-image=<NEW_IMAGE>

```

> Replace <NEW_IMAGE> with the desired version.

upgrade.title: Post-Upgrade Verification
upgrade.description:
This step verifies that the upgrade was successful.

```bash

oc get nodes

```

> Ensure all nodes are in 'Ready' state.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <IP>:<PORT>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the OpenShift API server is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the OpenShift services and ensure they are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Deployment" in version "apps/v1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually means that the API version is not supported in the current cluster version.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you are using the correct API version for your OpenShift version.

**Affected versions:** <= 4.14

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU for pod'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that there are not enough resources available to schedule the pod.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or adjust resource requests for your pods.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified image cannot be pulled from the registry.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure that the image exists in the specified registry.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new storage classes, improved security features, and enhanced monitoring capabilities.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 4.16, ensure to migrate any legacy networking configurations to the new supported plugins.

resolution.summary: You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'oc get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
